notes: Post-patch013 + post-patch014 with turboquant KV. **Numbers are dramatically lower than the 2026-04-14 fp8 baseline (results.json: 14.3 tok/s @128) because the 4-14 run pre-dates patch 013 — DeltaNet cache wiring was broken, so the layers were skipping recurrent computation and emitting fluent garbage at high speed.** Patch 013 fixed correctness (MMLU 16.7→93%); now all 24 DeltaNet + 8 standard-attn layers actually compute. Tail (128K/200K/250K) re-run with bench-script-timeout bumped to 1h. Note bench_long_context.py's prompt-text generator under-tokenizes vs the requested input_len: 250K request → only 85K actual tokens; the underlying observation is decode TPOT roughly flat from 128K→200K (~11.3 s) and rises to ~15 s at 250K, suggesting the dense weight read still dominates over KV reads at this token-count regime.
compared_to_qwen36: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B (db317e9) hit 0.9 tok/s @64K — Qwen3.5-27B at 0.2 tok/s @64K is 4x slower despite being smaller. Same DeltaNet-hybrid arch but Qwen3.6 is MoE (only 3B active params per token), Qwen3.5 is dense, so dense weight reads dominate at long context. README perf table updated to reflect this.
